content_of_post_to_comment: I am sitting at my window sill writing and drinking a coffee. I am watching a man tenderly pat and then kiss his little dog. He has taken it to the clearing outside my apartment at 8am in the morning to run laps in the cool air before it gets to 30 degrees again. I have never seen such love. He is picking bindis off its precious little body. His little driend has run away with its lead and is running laps around him like a tiny wind up toy. This has just resulted in more pats. Adorable.
comment: There’s something interesting about how this kind of tenderness often appears in small routines rather than grand gestures. Maybe love is less about intensity and more about the repeated attention we choose to give.
